| `wsl` | runtime substrate | **nameplate** (Director-dictated) | charge-time provision (deferred) |
| `podvm-*` | runtime substrate | **nameplate** (Director-dictated) | charge-time provision (deferred) |

The election *mechanism* differs even where the *location* is the same — `bole` election is **derived-pull** (the vessel reaches for the resolved coordinate the capture-file reports; capture stays pure and writes nothing), `reliquary` election is an **explicit yoke-stamp** across vessels — and the rename map must preserve that distinction (do not collapse derived-pull-ANCHOR and yoke-stamp-RELIQUARY because both land in the vessel). Nameplate is **not** a touchmark site for the build-time kinds; for the runtime-substrate kinds it *is* the election site, under Director authority, per the determinism premise. The charge-time provisioning mechanism for substrate touchmarks is consumption-side and deferred this heat (see Heat nature).

## Lode registry layout & naming (locked)

GAR category is **`rbi_ld`** (2-char, consistent with `rbi_hm`/`rq`/`es`/`df`). One Lode = one GAR `package` named **`rbi_ld/<kind-letter><stamp>`**, and that package *is* the atomic-delete unit. Kind-letters: `b` bole, `r` reliquary, `w` wsl, `vw`/`vn` podvm-{wsl,native} (`v`=VM, echoing the retired `rbv_PodmanVM.sh`; `b=bole` shares a letter with hallmark `b=bind` but the namespaces are separate, so it's accepted). Stamp matches the hallmark second-granular form (`YYMMDDHHMMSS`).

Members and provenance ride as **tags within that one package — never `/`-path-segments.** GAR has no subtree delete, so a slash makes sibling packages that can't be removed atomically (the exact regression today's reliquary `rbi_rq/<date>/<tool>` layout suffers). `packages delete rbi_ld/<kind><stamp>` removes the whole Lode in one call; per-member cleanup is `tags delete` / `versions delete`. ("Atomic" = single operation, not transactional rollback; partial-delete fixtures still assert member absence.) If GAR ever refuses a single-call delete of a mixed-artifact package, the fallback is a GCP-run delete loop over the package's tags — effectively atomic for our purposes.

**The sprue.** `rbi_*` is RB's reserved tag prefix; the Director's semantic names take everything else (RB refuses Director tags beginning `rbi_`). The sprue marks strings from **RB's domain** — RB's authored lexicon (`bole`, `vouch`, `sha256`, the reliquary tool words) **and** RB-measured-from-content values (the digest). It does **not** mark **foreign-cued** strings — anything derived from the vessel ORIGIN, the nameplate, or Director input. Hence `rbi_<name>` is forbidden (the name is a vessel cue) while `rbi_sha256-<digest>` is fine (the digest is RB-measured).

Member tags:

- **bole** (singleton): `:rbi_bole` (uniform greppable handle) + `:rbi_sha256-<full-hex>` (canonical OCI digest — matches what every tool reports, exact cross-Lode dedup) + `:<sanitized-origin>-<sha10>` (UNSPRUED — origin is a vessel cue; name + glance-fingerprint, = today's enshrine anchor, so cutover is a near-rename) + 0..N Director semantic names (unsprued) + `:rbi_vouch`.
- **reliquary** (cohort): `:rbi_<tool>` per member + `:rbi_vouch` (closed RB vocabulary; no Director/digest layer — kept clean).

**Provenance envelope (`:rbi_vouch`).** One per Lode, batch-level — the prototype brand-file promoted from a consumption-time text file to an acquisition-time artifact. One canonical home: the in-GAR `:rbi_vouch` tag (the Lode self-describing in the registry). The host-side capture handoff that election's derived-pull reads now emits only bare single-form chaining facts (a touchmark value-fact + a kind-brand enum) — it carries no provenance content, so reading host-side provenance now costs a GAR call (accepted; see the fact-chaining premise). Fields: lode identity (kind/package), `acquired_at`, `acquired_by` (Cloud Build SA), `capture_build` (the capture's own provenance), `trust_grade`, and `members[]` each carrying `name`/`origin`/`digest`/`verification`/its assigned `tags` — so the Director's naming is itself attested. `members[]` is the cardinality axis (length 1 for singletons, N for cohorts — same shape). Near-term unsigned (`signature: null`) but `schema`-versioned; future signing via cosign or the OCI referrers API (reserved-tag now, referrers once GAR maturity is confirmed). Exact field serialization is pace-time, firmed against `fast`-test fixtures — the shape above is the lock.

**Two trust grades, declared per Lode — Pale honesty, never over-claim:**

- **verified-against-published** (bole/reliquary/wsl): bytes re-checkable against a *persistent* upstream — OCI content-address on a durable registry, or an out-of-band published checksum (Canonical's SHA-256 for `wsl`).
- **recorded-at-acquisition** (podvm-*): upstream offers no durable re-checkable reference (quay rotates podvm out within days), so RB attests only the digest observed at capture — trust-on-first-acquisition.

## Kinds

| Kind | Members | Upstream source | Digest grade |
|------|---------|-----------------|--------------|
| `bole` | 1 | upstream OCI registry, consumed as FROM line | verified |
| `reliquary` | N | date-cohort of build-tool images from OCI registries (replaces today's reliquary noun) | verified per member |
| `wsl` | 1 | vendor-published rootfs tarball over HTTPS (e.g. Canonical cloud-images) | verified |
| `podvm-wsl` | N | `quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl` — platform fan-out, consumed by Windows podman | recorded |
| `podvm-native` | N | `quay.io/podman/machine-os` — platform fan-out, consumed by macOS/Linux podman | recorded |

Capture verb per kind (all capital-colophon `rbw-l*`, GAR-blob creators): `bole`→`ensconce` (`lE`), `reliquary`→`conclave` (`lC`), `wsl`→`underpin` (`lU`), `podvm-wsl`+`podvm-native`→`immure` (`lI`, one verb, quay archive as argument). Five kinds, four capture verbs.

Member count is the only structural variable — every kind is "a GAR package holding 1..N members plus batch provenance." `wsl` and `podvm-wsl` both touch WSL but are entirely distinct: `wsl` is the Linux distro rootfs that hosts everything on the Windows workload, `podvm-wsl` is the podman machine image that runs *inside* that distro. Names kept visibly different.

Payload shapes (orthogonal to member count): native layered OCI image (`bole`, `reliquary`) vs opaque-blob-wrapped-as-OCI (`wsl`, `podvm-*` — a rootfs tar or disk blob in a scratch/oras wrapper). The provenance envelope rides uniformly on both.

## Carried forward / consequences

- **caparison-w / garrison-w for the `wsl` kind.** The workload's `rbtww-main` is registered via `wsl --import` from a seed tarball; today that seed is created by `wsl --export` of admin's installed distro (GarrisonWsl). Under the `wsl` kind, that export path retires — the seed becomes a verified artifact pulled from GAR. The pre-existing revert pace in ₣A- targeting the WSL-stage DEV CACHE shortcut becomes obsolete and wants dropping or transferring when this heat commits to development.
- **Windows onboarding is stale and deferred.** The `rbhw*` Windows handbook tracks (Docker Desktop, context discipline) are stale and incomplete, and `wsl`/`podvm` consumption is deferred — so their onboarding has nothing live to teach. Lean: mark those tracks loudly NOT-available / deferred rather than delete, so the eventual `wsl`-kind consumption work rereads the host-side prose rather than reconstructing it. Onboarding follows *consumption*, not *acquisition*: the Lode acquisition unification does NOT unify the onboarding surface; substrate-kind onboarding lands in the host/Windows tracks, never in the Director build-lifecycle tracks.
- **`inscribe` is verb-overloaded — disambiguate, don't conflate.** `rbfl_inscribe` (reliquary tool-mirror, `rbw-dI`) is **in scope** — the operation absorbed into the `reliquary` kind's capture verb (`conclave`). `rbrd_inscribe` (depot tripwire, `rbw-rdi`) is a **different operation, out of scope, stays put**. There is no `mirror` verb to retire: `rbfd_mirror` is the bind vessel-mode copy (made-side, also out of scope). What is absorbed is `inscribe`/reliquary, not `mirror`.
- **Onboarding rename surface (tri-surfaced vs greenfield).** `bole`/`reliquary` are fully present across bash, adoc, and the Director onboarding tracks (`rbw-Odf`/`Oda`/`Odb`) — for these the cost is *rename* (only two typed linked terms, `RBYC_ENSHRINE` + `RBYC_RELIQUARY`, drive the typed references; the rest is hand-edited prose). `wsl`/`podvm-*` are surfaced *nowhere* today — for these the cost is *greenfield*, not rename. Do not estimate the onboarding work as uniform.
- **Refresh cadence** is per-kind and may diverge (podvm-wsl vs podvm-native).
- **Maintenance verbs stay in the `image` family (`rbw-i*`), deliberately.** `rekon` / `audit` / `wrest` / `jettison` survive intact and do **not** move into `rbw-l*`. They are a low-level backdoor — primitives for cleaning up bad things at the member/tag grain — and that's where they belong; the `l` family is for the first-class Lode lifecycle (capture, the read-only `divine` enumeration and `augur` inspection, and `banish`). The split from the maintenance verbs is **grain, not redundancy**: `divine` enumerates **Lodes** by touchmark and `augur` inspects **one Lode** (both lifecycle-grain, `rbw-l*`); `audit`/`rekon` enumerate **members/tags within** a Lode (maintenance-grain, `rbw-i*`). Their fetched-side domain variants (Reliquary + Enshrinement) consolidate under Lode/touchmark addressing; the made-side Hallmark variants are out of scope, untouched.
- **Tabtarget shape is decided, not open:** per-kind capture verbs under the new `l` (Lode) command family — `rbw-lE/lC/lU/lI` capture, `rbw-ld` divine (read-only show), `rbw-lB` banish — superseding the earlier "unified `rbw-dE` with a kind argument" framing. (These move out of the Depot `d` family where `enshrine`/`inscribe`/`yoke` live today.)
- **Election (`yoke` + the deferred substrate verb) lives with its consumer, NOT in `l` — lowercase, and does not commit.** Election mutates the *consumer* (vessel for build-time kinds, nameplate for substrate kinds), not the Lode — the touchmark is its *argument*, the consumer its *target* — so it belongs in the consumer's family, paralleling made-side hallmark election (which lives with the nameplate/kludge machinery, never with ark/hallmark). `l` stays reserved for GAR create/destroy of the Lode itself. Three axes separate election from `l`-capture: it targets the **consumer** (not the Lode); it is **lowercase** (cheap reversible config, not persistent GAR/cost state); and it is **operator-committed** — it writes the config and emits a "commit with your usual workflow" hint, never self-committing. That last is the near-universal RBK convention: `yoke` today is explicit — RBSDY, *"this primitive does not commit; the caller commits"* — and the only RBK bash command that self-commits is Marshal-Zero (`rblm`), a wholesale reset ceremony that still gates a clean tree first. Election also does **not self-gate** on a dirty tree; the clean-tree guard lives at the downstream consumer (the build reading the vessel, charge reading the nameplate — `rbob_bottle` already gates the nameplate clean). Pace-time: `yoke` moves out of Depot `d` and de-capitalizes (`rbw-dY` → a lowercase consumer-family colophon); exact destination letter is a layout choice. (Capture verbs raise no git question at all — they write GAR, not git-tracked config.)

## Discovery recipes — work to enumerate when paces approach

- **Verb redesign blast radius.** Scan `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSA*.adoc` and `RBSI*.adoc` for verbs touching captured artifacts; for each, classify per-kind variance (unchanged / split / retired). Yoke is the headline because consumer-landing differs per kind; other splits expected. Recipe, not enumeration — premature listing ages poorly. (Quoin anchors live in `RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`: `rbtgo_ark_enshrine`, `rbtgo_depot_inscribe`, `rbtgo_director_yoke`, `gar_enshrines_namespace`/`gar_reliquaries_namespace`, `rbst_reliquary_stamp`, `rbf_fact_reliquary` are the capture cluster.)
- **Substrate election landing.** For `wsl` and `podvm-*`, the touchmark is Director-pinned at the nameplate (determinism premise), materialized at charge — but *which* host-tier regime backs the node-side provisioning (station regime vs BURN node profile vs a new host-config regime) is open. Decide when the first non-vessel Lode consumption pace mounts.
- **Spec letter `RBSL`; code-module prefix `rbld` (ratified).** `L` verified free against the RBS* tree. The Lode *code* landed (post-₣BX) as the **`rbld*` family** — `rbld0_cli`/`rbld0_Lode` (CLI + 0-trick gestalt), `rblds_Spine`, `rbldb_Bole`, `rbldl_Lifecycle` — not a single module (constants `RBLD_*`, private `zrbld_*`), completing the system-wide Lode naming set: colophon `l` (`rbw-l*`), spec `RBSL`, GAR namespace `rbi_ld`, code `rbld`. This consciously relaxes the earlier "other than `rbl`" caution: `rbl` is a *pure container* — its only child is `rblm` Lifecycle Marshal, and it never itself names a thing — so terminal exclusivity holds and no coherent theme is diluted; `rbld_` and `rblm_` never collide at the 4-char grep grain. (`rbld_` code vs `rbi_ld` namespace are intentionally close — the module produces the namespace.)
- **GAR layout & provenance attachment — DECIDED** (see *Lode registry layout & naming*): single `rbi_ld`, one-package-per-Lode, members + reserved `:rbi_vouch` tag; `rbi_es`/`rbi_rq` migrate into `rbi_ld` at the deferred cutover. The OCI referrers API remains the future upgrade for signed attestation once GAR maturity is confirmed.

## Test coverage gate before deploy

Lifecycle fixtures against the new Lode surface must be slated and landed before deploy: capture / list / inspect / wrest / delete end-to-end against live GAR per kind, bookended by prereq-sweep at head and muster-absent at tail. The bar forks by kind: `bole`/`reliquary` carry their consumption-adjacent paths; `wsl`/`podvm` stop at the registry — **capture → list → inspect → per-member + whole-Lode delete against live GAR, no host in the loop** (consumption deferred). Whole-Lode delete is a single `packages delete` — the regression risk shifts to the **per-member delete path** (the cleanup-after-bugs case): multi-member kinds must carry concrete member-absence assertions after a partial delete, since that is where a package can be left in a partial state. The fixture surface scales with the operation surface; test paces are not optional polish. Manual operator testing during planning is the bridge, not a substitute.

**Capture-fidelity is a service-tier property, not a fast-tier one — settled on `bole`, holds for every kind.** The recorded digest is measured cloud-side (the in-pool `skopeo inspect | sha256` step), so an offline/deterministic test cannot verify `recorded == upstream` without either re-fetching upstream (the Pale — nondeterministic) or running the cloud-side script on the workstation (forbidden: cloud and local bash stay unshared). The live lifecycle fixture verifies fidelity *by construction* — the same `skopeo inspect` yields both the recorded value and the truth. So do **not** author a "fast fidelity" layer for any kind; it can only fake the check or test a reimplementation. What `fast` *could* honestly cover is host orchestration (right build composed, guards fire) — a different assertion needing a no-submit seam on the capture verb; deferred, not required. Empirical anchor: the `lode-lifecycle` service fixture (capture → enumerate → inspect → banish → restored) passed first-run against live GAR.

## Open issues before mounting paces

Remaining open items are pace-time choices, settled by the first relevant pace:

1. **Host-tier regime** for substrate touchmark election/provisioning (`wsl`/`podvm`) — station regime vs BURN node profile vs a new host-config regime. Decide when the first substrate-kind consumption pace mounts.

(GAR layout and provenance attachment, formerly open here, are now decided — see *Lode registry layout & naming*: single `rbi_ld`, one-package-per-Lode, reserved `:rbi_vouch` tag.)

## Heat nature

Design heat with the **bole kind landed**. Bole-kind capture (`ensconce`/`divine`/`banish`, the `rbld_` module + `rbi_ld` layout + the two cloud-side in-pool steps) is implemented and verified first-run against live GAR via the `lode-lifecycle` service fixture — it is the reusable shape every later kind copies. Remaining kinds (`reliquary`/`wsl`/`podvm`), the rename map, and election (`yoke`) are follow-up paces — **now unblocked: ₣BX's capture spine and host-module decomposition have landed (see Cross-heat dependency).** The bole `RBSL` cluster (RBSLE/D/A/B) and the shared RBS0 Lode quoin schema are also landed, leaving only the per-kind capture subdocs (RBSLC/U/I) to ride the verticals; the remaining landing-regime decisions defer until those commit, when everything here is re-investigated. Scope locked to the fetched side (made-images out of scope; see Shape) and to acquisition (wsl/podvm consumption deferred — this heat captures their bytes into GAR and proves control there; host consumption is the locked-but-deferred sequel).

## Cross-heat dependency — ₣BX built the capture spine (LANDED, gate discharged)

The remaining implementation work rides infrastructure the sibling heat **₣BX** (rbk-09-capture-machinery-unification) **built and landed** — not this one. ₣BX was a behavior-preserving refactor: it decomposed the host monoliths (`rbld`/`rbfl`/`rbfc`) into well-prefixed cluster files and stood up a **data-driven capture spine** (`rblds_Spine.sh`) plus a shared cloud step-library (`rbgjs/`), where a kind is a recipe (data) + a substitutions blob the spine consumes — no per-kind branch, no shared-file edit. ₣BX migrated bole and **reserved the per-kind body letters (`rbldr_`/`rbldw_`/`rbldv_`) — verified free** for this heat's kinds.

Consequence for this heat's shape: the "scaffold" the verticals were to ride is ₣BX's deliverable, **now landed**. Each kind here attaches as a **thin body file riding the spine — file-disjoint by construction** (the spine header is explicit: it owns no kind knowledge; recipe + substitutions are data), which dissolves the stub-vs-serialize question outright; this heat's own scaffold shrinks to the kind-registration surface ₣BX did **not** touch (colophons, kind-letter constants, the theurge registry, the divine-legend kinds-loop). ₣BX **preserved** the seams this heat needs: the capture step's pre-copy point stays clean for the ensconce collision guard, and inscribe/enshrine remain **live forks** (`RBSAE`, `rbfli_Inscribe.sh`) for this heat's cutovers to remove — and it relocated the chokepoints those cutovers repoint: **`zrbfc_resolve_tool_images` now lives in `rbfca_StepAssembly.sh`** (out of the former `rbfc` monolith).

**Sequencing (load-bearing — gate now satisfied):** the bole `RBSL` spec work was independent of ₣BX and landed first; everything else here was downstream of ₣BX. **₣BX has wrapped**, so the "mount only after ₣BX wraps" gate is **lifted** — the re-plan checkpoint and every implementation pace are mount-ready, re-baselined against ₣BX's landed layout (confirmed at this groom: spine registration-agnostic, body-letters free, RBSL cluster A/B/D/E present with C/U/I greenfield, cloud steps `rbgjl01`/`rbgjl02` + `rbgjs/` intact, enshrine/inscribe forks live). The constraint that guarded against regressing into the copy-paste forks ₣BX exists to abolish is discharged.

## References

- ₣AV (retired) — predecessor GAR-mirroring heat
- ₣Az (retired) — deferred ark-concept-debt heat, closed by the GAR-package substrate finding
- RBSAE `ark_enshrine` — current narrow-scope spec; becomes the `bole`-kind `ensconce` pipeline (`enshrine` the verb retires into per-kind capture)
- RBSAS `ark_summon`, RBSDI `depot_inscribe`, RBSDY `director_yoke` — verbs within blast radius
- RBSRV `RegimeVessel` — ORIGIN/ANCHOR precedent; enum-gated-variable-groups pattern
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh` + RBSPV — concrete podman-vm pipeline; reveals fan-out, the two-source quay split, the brand-file provenance embryo, and the ignite-VM machination that cloud-side acquisition dissolves
- BUSJCW CaparisonWindows, BUSJGW GarrisonWsl, BUSJIW InvigilateWindows — `wsl` kind consumption and the `wsl --export` seed path it retires
- WSL rootfs is vendor-published over HTTPS (Canonical cloud-images; Microsoft `DistributionInfo.json` carries per-distro URL + SHA-256) — basis for cloud-side `wsl` acquisition with no Windows in the loop
- GAR delete granularity (package / version / tag) verified — `packages delete` is the atomic whole-Lode delete unit
- CLAUDE.md "Prefix Naming Discipline" and "Quoin Sub-Letter Discipline"
- RBSHR-HorizonRoadmap.adoc — public-facing horizon entries

# jjezs_pace ₢BHAAK

## Character
Greenfield vertical, the largest — crane-based opaque-blob capture, ported from the GHCR prototype to GAR.

## Goal
Land the podvm (immure) capture machinery: port the podman-machine-image capture (crane manifest/blob navigation, FROM-scratch repackage) from the prototype (rbv_PodmanVM, RBSPV) onto the spine and into rbi_ld. This pace builds the single-family capture path; the two-family fan-out is the next pace.

## Cinched
- crane (not skopeo/docker) for opaque non-layered blobs; recorded-at-acquisition trust grade (quay publishes no durable checksum). Cloud-side acquisition. Ride the spine on the rbldv_ letter.
- Multi-member package holding a curated platform subset, never a full mirror (paddock podvm-selective premise). File-disjoint. Write RBSLI.

## Done
immure captures one quay family's curated platform set into rbi_ld with provenance; a theurge fixture proves capture -> divine -> banish live.

content.text:
Mounted ₣BH. Reading the paddock and the next pace.

**₢BHAAK — lode-podvm-capture-machinery:** land the `immure` (podvm) capture kind — port the podman-machine-image capture (crane manifest/blob navigation, FROM-scratch repackage) from the `rbv_PodmanVM`/RBSPV prototype onto the spine and into `rbi_ld`, with provenance and a live theurge fixture.

**Heat:** rbk-11-mvp-lode-universal-capture (10/19 done). The fetched-side capture model — per-kind verbs, one `Lode` package noun, the `rblds_` spine. The last two verticals landed `conclave` (reliquary, ₢BHAAI) and `underpin` (wsl, ₢BHAAJ); each kind attaches as a thin body file riding ₣BX's data-driven spine, file-disjoint.

This one is flagged the **largest greenfield vertical** and the first **structural outlier on trust**: crane (not skopeo/docker) for opaque non-layered blobs, and `recorded-at-acquisition` trust grade — quay publishes no durable checksum, so it's trust-on-first-acquisition, *not* verified-against-published like the three prior kinds. It also rides a new body letter, `rbldv_`.

**Cinched for this pace** (from the docket):
- crane for opaque blobs; recorded-at-acquisition grade; cloud-side acquisition; ride the spine on `rbldv_`.
- Multi-member package holding a **curated platform subset**, never a full mirror. File-disjoint. Write **RBSLI**.
- This pace builds the **single-family** capture path; the two-family fan-out (`machine-os-wsl` + `machine-os`) is the *next* pace.

**Done when:** `immure` captures one quay family's curated platform set into `rbi_ld` with provenance; a theurge fixture proves capture → divine → banish live.

The silks fits the docket — no rename needed. One flag: the pace is **[rough]**, though the docket body is actually substantive (Character/Goal/Cinched/Done all present).

**Recommended approach:** opus, sequential — this is architectural greenfield (crane navigation, opaque-blob repackage, a new trust grade) and shouldn't parallelize. Given [rough] + "the largest," I'd suggest a short groom/slate-refinement pass to firm the docket before cutting code — but the content may already be enough to mount directly.

Want me to **groom this pace first** (refine the docket, surface the open crane/curated-subset choices), or **mount it as-is** and start the vertical? I can also pull up the prototype (`rbv_PodmanVM`/RBSPV) or the prior `underpin` vertical as the copy-template before deciding.

Excellent! Now I have all the information. Let me create a comprehensive structured report:

---

## Complete Registration Map for RBK Lifecycle Test Fixtures

I've completed a thorough search of the RBK test infrastructure and found every registration site required for a new lifecycle fixture (e.g., `podvm-lifecycle`). Here's the complete map:

### 1. **FIXTURE STATIC DEFINITION** (in `rbtdrc_crucible.rs`)
   - **File:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs`
   - **Lines 2434-2448:** Three lifecycle fixture definitions show the pattern
   
   **wsl-lifecycle example (lines 2442-2448):**
   ```rust
   pub static RBTDRC_FIXTURE_WSL_LIFECYCLE: rbtdre_Fixture = rbtdre_Fixture {
       name: crate::rbtdrm_manifest::RBTDRM_FIXTURE_WSL_LIFECYCLE,
       disposition: rbtdre_Disposition::Independent,
       setup: None,
       teardown: None,
       cases: RBTDRC_CASES_WSL_LIFECYCLE,
   };
   ```
   
   **reliquary-lifecycle example (lines 2434-2440):**
   ```rust
   pub static RBTDRC_FIXTURE_RELIQUARY_LIFECYCLE: rbtdre_Fixture = rbtdre_Fixture {
       name: crate::rbtdrm_manifest::RBTDRM_FIXTURE_RELIQUARY_LIFECYCLE,
       disposition: rbtdre_Disposition::Independent,
       setup: None,
       teardown: None,
       cases: RBTDRC_CASES_RELIQUARY_LIFECYCLE,
   };
   ```

### 2. **CASES ARRAY DEFINITION** (in `rbtdrc_crucible.rs`)
   - **File:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs`
   - **Line 3265:** lode-lifecycle (2 cases)
   - **Line 3358:** reliquary-lifecycle (1 case)
   - **Line 3455:** wsl-lifecycle (1 case)
   
   **Pattern:**
   ```rust
   pub static RBTDRC_CASES_WSL_LIFECYCLE: &[rbtdre_Case] = &[case!(rbtdrc_wsl_lifecycle)];
   pub static RBTDRC_CASES_RELIQUARY_LIFECYCLE: &[rbtdre_Case] = &[case!(rbtdrc_reliquary_lifecycle)];
   pub static RBTDRC_CASES_LODE_LIFECYCLE: &[rbtdre_Case] = &[
       case!(rbtdrc_lode_lifecycle),
       case!(rbtdrc_lode_collision),
   ];
   ```

### 3. **SINGLE-FIXTURE LOOKUP REGISTRY (CRITICAL - THE BUG FIX)**
   - **File:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs`
   - **Lines 2470-2498:** `RBTDRC_FIXTURES` array — **THE REGISTRY THAT COMMIT 12f48cbab REVEALED AS MISSING**
   
   **Exact snippet (lines 2470-2498):**
   ```rust
   pub static RBTDRC_FIXTURES: &[&'static rbtdre_Fixture] = &[
       &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_TADMOR,
       &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_MORIAH,
       &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_SRJCL,
       &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_PLUML,
       &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_HALLMARK_LIFECYCLE,
       &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_LODE_LIFECYCLE,
       &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_RELIQUARY_LIFECYCLE,  // ← reliquary-lifecycle
       &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_WSL_LIFECYCLE,         // ← wsl-lifecycle
       &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_BATCH_VOUCH,
       &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_ACCESS_PROBE,
       // ... more fixtures ...
   ];
   ```
   
   **Purpose:** This array is directly consumed by `rbtdrc_lookup_fixture()` (line 2502), which `main.rs` uses to validate that `rbw-tf <name>` requests are bound to a Fixture. Without this entry, `rbw-tf podvm-lifecycle` reports "no Fixture static is bound" (main.rs line 137).

### 4. **MANIFEST CONSTANT** (String Boundary Discipline)
   - **File:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs`
   - **Lines 54-62:** Fixture name constants
   
   **Exact lines:**
   ```rust
   // Lode-lifecycle fixture — fetched-side base capture against live GAR:
   // ensconce -> divine (enumerate + inspect) -> banish, registry restored.
   pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_LIFECYCLE: &str = "lode-lifecycle";
   // Reliquary-lifecycle fixture — fetched-side cohort capture against live GAR:
   // conclave -> divine (enumerate + inspect members) -> banish, registry restored.
   pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_RELIQUARY_LIFECYCLE: &str = "reliquary-lifecycle";
   // Wsl-lifecycle fixture — fetched-side rootfs capture against live GAR:
   // underpin -> divine (enumerate + inspect rootfs member) -> banish, restored.
   pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_WSL_LIFECYCLE: &str = "wsl-lifecycle";
   ```

### 5. **REQUIRED COLOPHONS REGISTRY** (Manifest dependencies)
   - **File:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs`
   - **Lines 167-181:** Per-fixture colophon manifest
   
   **Exact entries:**

   ```rust
       RBTDGC_ENSCONCE_BOLE,

       RBTDGC_BANISH_LODE,
   ```

   **Purpose:** Declares the tabtargets (colophons) required to run each fixture. Missing entries would cause runtime failures.

### 6. **SUITE ARRAY ENTRIES** (Two suites for lifecycle fixtures)

   **a. "service" suite (lines 2532-2550):**
   - **File:** `rbtdrc_crucible.rs`
   - **Includes:** `&RBTDRC_FIXTURE_LODE_LIFECYCLE`, `&RBTDRC_FIXTURE_RELIQUARY_LIFECYCLE`, `&RBTDRC_FIXTURE_WSL_LIFECYCLE` (lines 2545-2547)
   - **Description:** "fast + GCP-credentialed bare fixtures"
   
   **Exact snippet (lines 2543-2549):**
   ```rust
   &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_ACCESS_PROBE,
   &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_HALLMARK_LIFECYCLE,
   &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_LODE_LIFECYCLE,         // ← lode-lifecycle in service suite
   &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_RELIQUARY_LIFECYCLE,    // ← reliquary-lifecycle in service suite
   &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_WSL_LIFECYCLE,          // ← wsl-lifecycle in service suite
   &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_BATCH_VOUCH,
   ```

   **b. "complete" suite (lines 2569-2590):**
   - **File:** `rbtdrc_crucible.rs`
   - **Includes:** Same three lifecycle fixtures (lines 2582-2584)
   - **Description:** "fast + every dependency-tiered fixture (service ∪ crucible)"
   
   **Exact snippet (lines 2580-2589):**

Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive report:

## Summary: Lode Kind Constants Architecture for RBK

Based on thorough analysis of the RBK codebase, here is the complete set of constants and patterns required for adding a new Lode capture kind.

### 1. Kind-Letter Scheme (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 210-226)

The kind-letter mappings are defined as:

```bash
# rbgc_Constants.sh (lines 222-226)
readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE="b"
readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_RELIQUARY="r"
readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_WSL="w"
readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_PODVM_WSL="vw"
readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_PODVM_NATIVE="vn"
```

The comment preceding this (lines 216-217) states:
```bash
# Kind letters (one per capture kind; podvm carries two for its quay families):
#   b  bole | r  reliquary | w  wsl | vw  podvm-wsl | vn  podvm-native
```

### 2. Kind-Brand Enumerations (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 228-235)

Brand strings are the kind enum values written to facts and read by consumers. Currently defined:

```bash
# rbgc_Constants.sh (lines 233-235)
readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_BOLE="bole"
readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY="reliquary"
readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_WSL="wsl"
```

The comment (lines 228-232) explains:
```bash
# Kind-brand enum — the touchmark's kind carried in the host-side single-form
# chaining fact a derived-pull election reads to resolve the member tag. It is
# read as its own fact, NOT parsed from the touchmark's kind-letter prefix
# (the chaining channel is single-form: opaque values, never parsed). Bole and
# reliquary land today; later kinds add their brand here with their vertical.
```

### 3. Member/Provenance Tag Constants (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 237-247)

**Universal tags used by all kinds:**

```bash
# rbgc_Constants.sh (lines 241-248)
readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE="rbi_"               # RB reserved tag prefix; member tags compose as <sprue><name>
readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE="rbi_bole"            # uniform greppable handle (bole singleton)
readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH="rbi_vouch"          # one-per-Lode provenance envelope
readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX="rbi_sha256-"  # canonical OCI digest tag: rbi_sha256-<full-hex>
# reliquary cohort members carry the clean scheme :<sprue><tool> (e.g. rbi_skopeo)
# — no digest/fingerprint layer; the tool name is RB-authored lexicon, so sprued.
readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_ROOTFS="rbi_rootfs"        # wsl singleton: the opaque rootfs blob member (RB-authored, sprued)
```

**Key patterns:**
- `RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH` is used by ALL kinds as the provenance envelope tag
- `RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX` is used by bole for OCI digest tracking
- `RBGC_LODE_TAG_ROOTFS` is WSL-specific for the opaque blob member
- Reliquary tool tags compose as `<sprue><tool>` where tool is sprued (e.g., `rbi_gcloud`, `rbi_skopeo`)

### 4. Trust and Provenance Constants (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 249-253)

```bash
# rbgc_Constants.sh (lines 251-253)
readonly RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED="verified-against-published"
readonly RBGC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED="recorded-at-acquisition"
readonly RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA="rbld-vouch-1"    # near-term unsigned, schema-versioned
```

Comment (lines 249-250):
```bash
# bole captures the durable-upstream grade; podvm-* will carry the recorded grade.
```

### 5. WSL-Kind Specific Constants (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 255-281)

```bash
# rbgc_Constants.sh (lines 275-281)
readonly RBGC_LODE_WSL_URL_TEMPLATE="https://cdimage.ubuntu.com/ubuntu-base/releases/%s/release/ubuntu-base-%s-base-%s.tar.gz"
readonly RBGC_LODE_WSL_ARCH_DEFAULT="amd64"
# Ubuntu CD Image Automatic Signing Key (2012, RSA4096), cdimage@ubuntu.com —
# signs cdimage SHA256SUMS.gpg. The trust anchor: tiny, stable, auditable. The
# cloud step fetches the key BY this fingerprint into a clean keyring, so the
# keyserver is never trusted — only a signature from exactly this key passes.
readonly RBGC_LODE_WSL_SIGNING_FPR="843938DF228D22F7B3742BC0D94AA3F0EFE21092"
```

### 6. GAR Categorical Namespace Constant (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 202-208)

The Lode namespace root:

```bash
# rbgc_Constants.sh (lines 208)
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_LODES="rbi_ld"
```

With context (lines 202-208):
```bash
# GAR Categorical Namespaces (₢A_AAK layout)
# Top-level namespaces under which arks are stored. Consumed by rbgl_GarLayout.sh.
# The hm/rq siblings hold Director-authored image families; rbi_df holds
# Payor-authored depot-scoped OCI artifacts produced during depot lifetime.
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS="rbi_hm"
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES="rbi_rq"
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS="rbi_df"
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_LODES="rbi_ld"
```

### 7. Lode Package Layout Pattern (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 210-214)

The architectural pattern:

```bash
# GAR Lode Layout (fetched-side universal capture — see RBSL)
# One Lode = one GAR package named rbi_ld/<kind-letter><stamp>; that package
# IS the atomic-delete unit (single `packages delete`). Members and provenance
# ride as TAGS within the one package, never as /-path-segments (GAR has no
# subtree delete). Stamp matches the hallmark second-granular form YYMMDDHHMMSS.
```

**Pattern:** `rbi_ld/<kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>:<member-tag>`
- Example: `rbi_ld/b260602120000:rbi_bole` (bole), `rbi_ld/r260602120000:rbi_docker` (reliquary tool), `rbi_ld/w260602120000:rbi_rootfs` (wsl)

### 8. Reliquary Tool Basenames (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 306-314)

```bash
# Reliquary Tool Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
# Canonical tool names under rbi_rq/<date>/. Authoritative
# manifest lives in rbgji/rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh.
readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD="gcloud"
readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER="docker"
readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE="alpine"
readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT="syft"
readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT="binfmt"
readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO="skopeo"
```

toolUseResult.prompt:
I'm adding a new Lode capture kind called `podvm` (podman VM machine image) to the RBK codebase. The existing Lode kinds are `bole`, `reliquary`, and `wsl`. I need to understand every constant/brand/tag a new kind requires in `Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh` and related constant files.

Please search `Tools/rbk/rbgc_Constants.sh` (and any other constants files like `rbcc_Constants.sh`, `rbgl_GarLayout.sh`) and report:

1. The kind-letter scheme for Lode kinds — find the constants that map each kind to its GAR kind-letter. The paddock says: `b` bole, `r` reliquary, `w` wsl, and `vw`/`vn` reserved for podvm-{wsl,native} where `v`=VM. Quote the exact existing constant definitions for bole/reliquary/wsl kind-letters and brands.

2. Every RBGC constant added for the `wsl` (underpin) kind and the `reliquary` (conclave) kind — brand strings, member tag names (e.g. `rbi_rootfs`, `rbi_<tool>`, `rbi_vouch`), URL templates, arch defaults, signing fingerprints, the `RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH` constant. Quote each with its surrounding context so I can see the naming pattern.

3. The `rbi_ld` GAR namespace constant and the package-naming pattern (`rbi_ld/<kind-letter><stamp>`).

4. The divine "legend" — where the kind-letter -> kind-name legend lines live (the paddock mentions "divine gains the w->wsl legend line"). Find this legend and quote the existing entries.

5. Any place that enumerates/lists the valid Lode kinds that a new kind would need to be added to.

Report as a structured list with file:line references and exact quoted constant definitions. Read-only — do not modify anything. Be thorough.

Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive report:

## Summary: Lode Kind Constants Architecture for RBK

Based on thorough analysis of the RBK codebase, here is the complete set of constants and patterns required for adding a new Lode capture kind.

### 1. Kind-Letter Scheme (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 210-226)

The kind-letter mappings are defined as:

```bash
# rbgc_Constants.sh (lines 222-226)
readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE="b"
readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_RELIQUARY="r"
readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_WSL="w"
readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_PODVM_WSL="vw"
readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_PODVM_NATIVE="vn"
```

The comment preceding this (lines 216-217) states:
```bash
# Kind letters (one per capture kind; podvm carries two for its quay families):
#   b  bole | r  reliquary | w  wsl | vw  podvm-wsl | vn  podvm-native
```

### 2. Kind-Brand Enumerations (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 228-235)

Brand strings are the kind enum values written to facts and read by consumers. Currently defined:

```bash
# rbgc_Constants.sh (lines 233-235)
readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_BOLE="bole"
readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY="reliquary"
readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_WSL="wsl"
```

The comment (lines 228-232) explains:
```bash
# Kind-brand enum — the touchmark's kind carried in the host-side single-form
# chaining fact a derived-pull election reads to resolve the member tag. It is
# read as its own fact, NOT parsed from the touchmark's kind-letter prefix
# (the chaining channel is single-form: opaque values, never parsed). Bole and
# reliquary land today; later kinds add their brand here with their vertical.
```

### 3. Member/Provenance Tag Constants (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 237-247)

**Universal tags used by all kinds:**

```bash
# rbgc_Constants.sh (lines 241-248)
readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE="rbi_"               # RB reserved tag prefix; member tags compose as <sprue><name>
readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE="rbi_bole"            # uniform greppable handle (bole singleton)
readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH="rbi_vouch"          # one-per-Lode provenance envelope
readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX="rbi_sha256-"  # canonical OCI digest tag: rbi_sha256-<full-hex>
# reliquary cohort members carry the clean scheme :<sprue><tool> (e.g. rbi_skopeo)
# — no digest/fingerprint layer; the tool name is RB-authored lexicon, so sprued.
readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_ROOTFS="rbi_rootfs"        # wsl singleton: the opaque rootfs blob member (RB-authored, sprued)
```

**Key patterns:**
- `RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH` is used by ALL kinds as the provenance envelope tag
- `RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX` is used by bole for OCI digest tracking
- `RBGC_LODE_TAG_ROOTFS` is WSL-specific for the opaque blob member
- Reliquary tool tags compose as `<sprue><tool>` where tool is sprued (e.g., `rbi_gcloud`, `rbi_skopeo`)

### 4. Trust and Provenance Constants (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 249-253)

```bash
# rbgc_Constants.sh (lines 251-253)
readonly RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED="verified-against-published"
readonly RBGC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED="recorded-at-acquisition"
readonly RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA="rbld-vouch-1"    # near-term unsigned, schema-versioned
```

Comment (lines 249-250):
```bash
# bole captures the durable-upstream grade; podvm-* will carry the recorded grade.
```

### 5. WSL-Kind Specific Constants (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 255-281)

```bash
# rbgc_Constants.sh (lines 275-281)
readonly RBGC_LODE_WSL_URL_TEMPLATE="https://cdimage.ubuntu.com/ubuntu-base/releases/%s/release/ubuntu-base-%s-base-%s.tar.gz"
readonly RBGC_LODE_WSL_ARCH_DEFAULT="amd64"
# Ubuntu CD Image Automatic Signing Key (2012, RSA4096), cdimage@ubuntu.com —
# signs cdimage SHA256SUMS.gpg. The trust anchor: tiny, stable, auditable. The
# cloud step fetches the key BY this fingerprint into a clean keyring, so the
# keyserver is never trusted — only a signature from exactly this key passes.
readonly RBGC_LODE_WSL_SIGNING_FPR="843938DF228D22F7B3742BC0D94AA3F0EFE21092"
```

### 6. GAR Categorical Namespace Constant (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 202-208)

The Lode namespace root:

```bash
# rbgc_Constants.sh (lines 208)
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_LODES="rbi_ld"
```

With context (lines 202-208):
```bash
# GAR Categorical Namespaces (₢A_AAK layout)
# Top-level namespaces under which arks are stored. Consumed by rbgl_GarLayout.sh.
# The hm/rq siblings hold Director-authored image families; rbi_df holds
# Payor-authored depot-scoped OCI artifacts produced during depot lifetime.
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS="rbi_hm"
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES="rbi_rq"
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS="rbi_df"
readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_LODES="rbi_ld"
```

### 7. Lode Package Layout Pattern (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 210-214)

The architectural pattern:

```bash
# GAR Lode Layout (fetched-side universal capture — see RBSL)
# One Lode = one GAR package named rbi_ld/<kind-letter><stamp>; that package
# IS the atomic-delete unit (single `packages delete`). Members and provenance
# ride as TAGS within the one package, never as /-path-segments (GAR has no
# subtree delete). Stamp matches the hallmark second-granular form YYMMDDHHMMSS.
```

**Pattern:** `rbi_ld/<kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>:<member-tag>`
- Example: `rbi_ld/b260602120000:rbi_bole` (bole), `rbi_ld/r260602120000:rbi_docker` (reliquary tool), `rbi_ld/w260602120000:rbi_rootfs` (wsl)

### 8. Reliquary Tool Basenames (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 306-314)

```bash
# Reliquary Tool Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
# Canonical tool names under rbi_rq/<date>/. Authoritative
# manifest lives in rbgji/rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh.
readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD="gcloud"
readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER="docker"
readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE="alpine"
readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT="syft"
readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT="binfmt"
readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO="skopeo"
```

These constants are used to compose member tags in reliquary Lodes as `rbi_<tool>`.

### 9. Lode Chaining Facts (rbgc_Constants.sh, lines 324-331)

### 10. GAR Layout Integration (rbgl_GarLayout.sh, lines 40-50)

The layout module derives its constants from rbgc:

```bash
readonly RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT="${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES}"
readonly RBGL_DEPOT_FACTS_ROOT="${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_DEPOT_FACTS}"

```

### 11. The Divine Legend (rbldl_Lifecycle.sh, lines 54-62)

The "divine gains the w->wsl legend line" mentioned in the paddock — the human-readable legend printed when enumerating Lodes:

```bash
# rbldl_Lifecycle.sh (lines 54-62)
# Kind-letter legend, printed once so rows carry no repeated per-row column.
# A touchmark's leading letter is its kind (b260602075327 -> bole); the
# reader decodes the prefix from this key. One entry per implemented kind.
local -r z_kind_fmt="    %-3s %-10s %s\n"
echo ""
printf "  Kinds (touchmark prefix):\n"
printf "${z_kind_fmt}" "${RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE}"      "bole"      "upstream OCI image, consumed as a FROM line"
printf "${z_kind_fmt}" "${RBGC_LODE_KIND_RELIQUARY}" "reliquary" "date-cohort of build-tool images"
printf "${z_kind_fmt}" "${RBGC_LODE_KIND_WSL}"       "wsl"       "vendor-published rootfs tarball, opaque-blob member"
```

**Places to add for podvm:** Add two lines here for `vw` and `vn` with descriptions.

### 12. Brand Case Dispatch (rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, context available)

The only current validation case statement (lines 185-190 region in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh):

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
3	#
4	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	#
8	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	#
10	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	# limitations under the License.
15	#
16	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
17	#
18	# Recipe Bottle VM - Podman Virtual Machine Management
19	
20	######################################################################
21	# WARNING: This implementation targets GHCR (GitHub Container Registry).
22	# It has NOT been ported to Google Artifact Registry (GAR/Depot).
23	# The operations are functionally correct but the registry target is
24	# obsolete for this project.
25	# See lenses/RBSPV-PodmanVmSupplyChain.adoc for context.
26	######################################################################
27	
28	set -euo pipefail
29	
30	# Multiple inclusion detection
31	test -z "${ZRBV_INCLUDED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbv multiply included - check sourcing hierarchy"
32	ZRBV_INCLUDED=1
33	
34	######################################################################
35	# Internal Functions (zrbv_*)
36	
37	zrbv_kindle() {
38	  # Build module variables from furnished environment (already validated by CLI)
39	
40	  # Module Variables (ZRBV_*)
41	  readonly ZRBV_GIT_REGISTRY="ghcr.io"
42	
43	  readonly ZRBV_IGNITE_INIT_STDOUT="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/ignite_init_stdout.txt"
44	  readonly ZRBV_IGNITE_INIT_STDERR="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/ignite_init_stderr.txt"
45	  readonly ZRBV_DEPLOY_INIT_STDOUT="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/deploy_init_stdout.txt"
46	  readonly ZRBV_DEPLOY_INIT_STDERR="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/deploy_init_stderr.txt"
47	
48	  readonly ZRBV_FACT_DIGEST_PREFIX="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/fact-digest-"
49	  readonly ZRBV_FACT_SOURCE_PREFIX="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/fact-source-"
50	  readonly ZRBV_FACT_EXTENSION_PREFIX="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/fact-extension-"
51	  readonly ZRBV_FACT_ARCH_PREFIX="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/fact-arch-"
52	  readonly ZRBV_FACT_DISKTYPE_PREFIX="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/fact-disktype-"
53	
54	  readonly ZRBV_EMPLACED_BRAND_FILE="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/emplaced_brand_file.txt"
55	  readonly ZRBV_GENERATED_BRAND_FILE="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/generated_brand_file.txt"
56	  readonly ZRBV_NATURAL_TAG_FILE="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/natural_tag.txt"
57	
58	  readonly ZRBV_PODMAN_REMOVE_PREFIX="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/podman_inspect_remove_"
59	  readonly ZRBV_IDENTITY_TS_FILE="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/identity_timestamp.txt"
60	  readonly ZRBV_MOW_MANIFEST_JSON="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/mow_manifest.json"
61	  readonly ZRBV_MOS_MANIFEST_JSON="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/mos_manifest.json"
62	  readonly ZRBV_MOW_ENTRIES_JSON="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/mow_entries.json"
63	  readonly ZRBV_MOS_ENTRIES_JSON="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/mos_entries.json"
64	  readonly ZRBV_MOW_DECODED_PREFIX="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/mow_decoded_"
65	  readonly ZRBV_MOS_DECODED_PREFIX="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/mos_decoded_"
66	
67	  readonly ZRBV_VM_TEMP_DIR="/tmp/rbv-upload"
68	  readonly ZRBV_VM_MANIFEST_PREFIX="${ZRBV_VM_TEMP_DIR}/manifest_"
69	  readonly ZRBV_VM_BLOB_PREFIX="${ZRBV_VM_TEMP_DIR}/blob_"
70	
71	  readonly ZRBV_BLOB_INFO="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/blob_info.txt"
72	  readonly ZRBV_LAYERS_JSON="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/layers.json"
73	
74	  readonly ZRBV_VMIMAGE_TAG_PREFIX="${ZRBV_GIT_REGISTRY}/${RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER}/${RBRR_REGISTRY_NAME}:podvm-${RBRM_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION}-"
75	
76	  # Additional temp file paths
77	  readonly ZRBV_SCRIPT_DIR="$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")"
78	
79	  readonly ZRBV_KINDLED=1
80	}
81	
82	zrbv_sentinel() {
83	  # Guard that kindle ran before function execution
84	  test "${ZRBV_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbv not kindled - call zrbv_kindle first"
85	}
86	
87	# Generate brand file content
88	zrbv_generate_brand_file() {
89	  test ! -f "${ZRBV_GENERATED_BRAND_FILE}" || buc_die 'Brand file already exists'
90	
91	  {
92	    echo "# Recipe Bottle VM Brand File"
93	    echo "#"
94	    echo "PODMAN_VERSION: ${RBRM_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION}"
95	    echo "VMIMAGE_ORIGIN: ${RBRM_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN}"
96	    echo "VMIMAGE_FQIN:   ${RBRM_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_FQIN}"
97	    echo "VMIMAGE_DIGEST: ${RBRM_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_DIGEST}"
98	    echo "IDENTITY:       ${RBRM_CHOSEN_IDENTITY}"
99	  } > "${ZRBV_GENERATED_BRAND_FILE}"
100	}
101	
102	# Extract natural tag from podman init output
103	zrbv_extract_natural_tag() {
104	  local z_init_output_file="$1"
105	
106	  local z_init_line
107	  local z_tag
108	  while IFS= read -r z_init_line; do
109	    case "${z_init_line}" in *"Looking up Podman Machine image at"*)
110	      z_tag="${z_init_line#*Looking up Podman Machine image at }"
111	      z_tag="${z_tag% to create VM*}"
112	      printf '%s\n' "${z_tag}"
113	      break
114	    ;; esac
115	  done < "${z_init_output_file}" > "${ZRBV_NATURAL_TAG_FILE}"
116	
117	  test -s "${ZRBV_NATURAL_TAG_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to extract natural tag from init output"
118	}

120	# Compare two files and return error code if different
121	zrbv_is_different_predicate() {
122	  local z_file1="$1"
123	  local z_file2="$2"
124	
125	  local z_content1="$(<"${z_file1}")"
126	  local z_content2="$(<"${z_file2}")"
127	
128	  if test "${z_content1}" = "${z_content2}"; then
129	    return 0
130	  else
131	    buc_warn "File content mismatch detected!"
132	    buc_warn "File 1 (${z_file1}) contents:"
133	    echo "${z_content1}"
134	    buc_warn "File 2 (${z_file2}) contents:"
135	    echo "${z_content2}"
136	    return 1
137	  fi
138	}

140	# Stop and remove a VM if it exists
141	zrbv_remove_vm() {
142	  local z_vm_name="$1"
143	
144	  if podman machine inspect "${z_vm_name}" > "${ZRBV_PODMAN_REMOVE_PREFIX}${z_vm_name}.txt"; then
145	    buc_info       "Stopping ${z_vm_name}..."
146	    podman machine stop     "${z_vm_name}" || buc_warn "Failed to stop ${z_vm_name} during _remove_vm"
147	    buc_info       "Removing ${z_vm_name}..."
148	    podman machine rm -f    "${z_vm_name}" || buc_die "Failed to remove ${z_vm_name}"
149	  else
150	    buc_info             "VM ${z_vm_name} does not exist. Nothing to remove."
151	  fi
152	}

154	# Prepare ignite machine: start if present, or fully reinit if force_reinit=true
155	zrbv_ignite_bootstrap() {
156	  local z_force_reinit="${1}"
157	
158	  buc_info "Bootstrapping ignite machine: ${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}"
159	
160	  if podman machine inspect "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" &>/dev/null; then
161	    buc_info "Stopping existing ignite machine (if running)..."
162	    podman machine stop "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" 2>/dev/null \
163	      || buc_die "Could not stop existing ignite VM cleanly"
164	  fi
165	
166	  if test "${z_force_reinit}" = "true"; then
167	    buc_step "Removing existing ignite machine due to force_reinit..."
168	    zrbv_remove_vm "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" || buc_die "Removal failed."
169	  fi
170	
171	  if ! podman machine inspect "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" &>/dev/null; then
172	    buc_step "Creating ignite VM with natural podman init..."
173	    podman machine init --log-level=debug      "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" \
174	                                            2> "${ZRBV_IGNITE_INIT_STDERR}"  \
175	         | "${ZRBV_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbupmis_Scrub.sh" "${ZRBV_IGNITE_INIT_STDOUT}"  \
176	      || buc_die "Bad init."
177	
178	    buc_step "Starting ignite machine..."
179	    podman machine start "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" || buc_die "Failed to start ignite machine"
180	
181	    buc_step "Installing crane..."
182	    podman machine ssh "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" --                        \
183	        "curl -sL ${RBRM_CRANE_TAR_GZ} | sudo tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin crane" \
184	        || buc_die "crane fail"
185	
186	    buc_step "Verify crane installation..."
187	    podman machine ssh "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" -- \
188	        "crane version" || buc_die "crane confirm fail."
189	
190	    buc_step "Installing tools..."
191	    podman machine ssh "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" -- \
192	        "sudo dnf install -y jq" || buc_die "tools install fail"
193	  else
194	    buc_step "Restarting ignite machine..."
195	    podman machine start "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" || buc_die "Failed to restart ignite machine"
196	  fi
197	
198	  buc_step "Ignite machine ready with crane installed"
199	}

201	# Helper function to process one image type
202	zrbv_process_image_type() {
203	  local z_manifest_file="$1"
204	  local z_entries_file="$2"
205	  local z_decoded_prefix="$3"
206	  local z_prefix="$4"
207	  local z_fqin="$5"
208	  local z_family_name="$6"
209	
210	  buc_step "Retrieving manifest for ${z_family_name} image: ${z_fqin}"
211	  podman machine ssh "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" --        \
212	        "crane manifest ${z_fqin} | jq ." > "${z_manifest_file}" \
213	      || buc_die "Failed to retrieve ${z_family_name} manifest"
214	
215	  buc_step "${z_family_name} Manifest:"
216	  cat "${z_manifest_file}"
217	
218	  buc_step "Extract manifest entries for ${z_family_name}..."
219	  jq -r '.manifests[] | @base64' "${z_manifest_file}" > "${z_entries_file}" \
220	    || buc_die "Failed to extract ${z_family_name} entries"
221	
222	  buc_step "${z_family_name} family images:"
223	  local z_entry_num=0
224	  while IFS= read -r z_entry; do
225	    z_entry_num=$((z_entry_num + 1))
226	    local       z_decoded="${z_decoded_prefix}${z_entry_num}.json"
227	    local     z_arch_file="${z_decoded_prefix}${z_entry_num}_arch.txt"
228	    local z_disktype_file="${z_decoded_prefix}${z_entry_num}_disktype.txt"
229	    local   z_digest_file="${z_decoded_prefix}${z_entry_num}_digest.txt"
230	
231	    openssl enc -base64 -d <<< "${z_entry}"  > "${z_decoded}"
232	    jq -r '.platform.architecture'          "${z_decoded}" > "${z_arch_file}"
233	    jq -r '.annotations.disktype // "base"' "${z_decoded}" > "${z_disktype_file}"
234	    jq -r '.digest'                         "${z_decoded}" > "${z_digest_file}"
235	
236	    local z_arch z_disktype z_digest
237	    z_arch=$(<"${z_arch_file}")
238	    z_disktype=$(<"${z_disktype_file}")
239	    z_digest=$(<"${z_digest_file}")
240	    local z_platform_spec="${z_prefix}_${z_arch}_${z_disktype}"
241	
242	    echo "${z_digest}"      > "${ZRBV_FACT_DIGEST_PREFIX}${z_platform_spec}"
243	    echo "${z_fqin}"        > "${ZRBV_FACT_SOURCE_PREFIX}${z_platform_spec}"
244	    echo "${z_arch}"        > "${ZRBV_FACT_ARCH_PREFIX}${z_platform_spec}"
245	    echo "${z_disktype}"    > "${ZRBV_FACT_DISKTYPE_PREFIX}${z_platform_spec}"
246	
247	    echo "  ${ZRBV_GIT_REGISTRY}/${RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER}/${RBRR_REGISTRY_NAME}:podvm-${RBRM_CHOSEN_IDENTITY}-${RBRM_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION}-${z_platform_spec}"
248	  done < "${z_entries_file}"
249	}

251	######################################################################
252	# External Functions (rbv_*)
253	
254	rbv_nuke() {
255	  # Name parameters
256	  local z_yes_opt="${1:-}"
257	
258	  # Handle documentation mode
259	  buc_doc_brief "Completely reset the podman virtual machine environment"
260	  buc_doc_oparm "yes_opt" "If 'YES', then skip confirmation step"
261	  buc_doc_lines "Destroys ALL containers, VMs, and VM cache"
262	  buc_doc_lines "Requires explicit YES confirmation"
263	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
264	
265	  # Ensure module started
266	  zrbv_sentinel
267	
268	  # Perform command
269	  buv_dir_exists "${RBRS_PODMAN_ROOT_DIR}" || buc_warn "Podman directory not found."
270	
271	  buc_step "WARNING: This will destroy all podman VMs and cache found in ${RBRS_PODMAN_ROOT_DIR}"
272	
273	  # Skip confirmation if YES was passed as parameter
274	  test "${z_yes_opt}" = "YES" || buc_require "This will destroy all podman VMs and cache!" "YES"
275	
276	  buc_step "Stopping all containers..."
277	  podman stop -a  || buc_warn "Attempt to stop all containers failed; normal if machine not started."
278	
279	  buc_step "Removing all containers..."
280	  podman rm -a -f || buc_warn "Attempt to remove all containers failed; normal if machine not started."
281	
282	  buc_step "Removing all podman machines..."
283	  for z_vm in $(podman machine list -q); do
284	    z_vm="${z_vm%\*}"  # Remove trailing asterisk indicating 'current vm'
285	    zrbv_remove_vm "${z_vm}" || buc_die "Attempt to remove VM ${z_vm} failed."
286	  done
287	
288	  buc_step "Deleting VM cache directory..."
289	  rm -rf "${RBRS_PODMAN_ROOT_DIR}/machine"/* || buc_die "Failed to clear VM machine cache"
290	
291	  buc_success "Podman VM environment reset complete"
292	}

294	# Mirror VM images to GHCR
295	rbv_mirror() {
296	  # Handle documentation mode
297	  buc_doc_brief "Process manifests for WSL and standard machine-os images, then upload needed disk images to GHCR"
298	  buc_doc_lines "Queries quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl and quay.io/podman/machine-os"
299	  buc_doc_lines "Uses crane manifest to retrieve raw manifests, formatted with jq"
300	  buc_doc_lines "Validates that all RBRM_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS are available"
301	  buc_doc_lines "Downloads needed disk images and packages them as container images"
302	  buc_doc_lines "Builds all images locally, creates local manifest list, then single push to GHCR"
303	  buc_doc_lines "Tags each platform image individually for direct access"
304	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
305	
306	  # Ensure module started
307	  zrbv_sentinel
308	
309	  # Perform command
310	  buc_step "Prepare fresh ignite machine with crane and tools..."
311	  zrbv_ignite_bootstrap false || buc_die "Failed to create temp machine"
312	
313	  buc_step "Generate new identity for this build..."
314	  date +'%Y%m%d-%H%M%S' > "${ZRBV_IDENTITY_TS_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to generate identity timestamp"
315	  local z_new_identity
316	  z_new_identity=$(<"${ZRBV_IDENTITY_TS_FILE}")
317	  test -n "${z_new_identity}" || buc_die "Empty identity timestamp from ${ZRBV_IDENTITY_TS_FILE}"
318	  buc_info "New identity: ${z_new_identity}"
319	
320	  buc_step "Potential container image names for caching:"
321	
322	  # Process each image type
323	  zrbv_process_image_type        \
324	    "${ZRBV_MOW_MANIFEST_JSON}"  \
325	    "${ZRBV_MOW_ENTRIES_JSON}"   \
326	    "${ZRBV_MOW_DECODED_PREFIX}" \
327	    "mow"                        \
328	    "quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl:${RBRM_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION}" \
329	    "Machine-OS-WSL"
330	
331	  zrbv_process_image_type        \
332	    "${ZRBV_MOS_MANIFEST_JSON}"  \
333	    "${ZRBV_MOS_ENTRIES_JSON}"   \
334	    "${ZRBV_MOS_DECODED_PREFIX}" \
335	    "mos"                        \
336	    "quay.io/podman/machine-os:${RBRM_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION}" \
337	    "Machine-OS standard"
338	
339	  buc_step "Validating RBRM_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS availability..."
340	
341	  local z_missing_images=""
342	  for z_needed_image in ${RBRM_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS}; do
343	    if test ! -f "${ZRBV_FACT_DIGEST_PREFIX}${z_needed_image}"; then
344	      z_missing_images="${z_missing_images} ${z_needed_image}"
345	    else
346	      buc_info "Found needed image: ${z_needed_image}"
347	    fi
348	  done
349	
350	  test -z "${z_missing_images}" || buc_die "Missing required disk images:${z_missing_images}"
351	
352	  buc_step "All needed disk images are available in upstream manifests"
353	
354	  buc_step "Setting up VM temporary dir..."
355	  podman machine ssh "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" --              \
356	      "rm -rf ${ZRBV_VM_TEMP_DIR} && mkdir -p ${ZRBV_VM_TEMP_DIR}" \
357	    || buc_die "Failed to create VM temp directory"
358	
359	  buc_step "Building container images..."
360	  for z_needed_image in ${RBRM_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS}; do
361	    buc_step "Processing: ${z_needed_image}"
362	
363	    # Read facts
364	    local z_digest z_source_fqin
365	    z_digest=$(<"${ZRBV_FACT_DIGEST_PREFIX}${z_needed_image}")
366	    z_source_fqin=$(<"${ZRBV_FACT_SOURCE_PREFIX}${z_needed_image}")
367	
368	    podman machine ssh "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" --                                       \
369	        "crane manifest ${z_source_fqin}@${z_digest} | jq -r '.layers[]'" > "${ZRBV_LAYERS_JSON}" \
370	      || buc_die "Failed to extract layers for ${z_needed_image}"
371	
372	    jq -r 'select(.annotations."org.opencontainers.image.title" // .mediaType | test("disk|raw|tar|qcow2|machine")) | .digest + ":" + .mediaType' \
373	           "${ZRBV_LAYERS_JSON}" > "${ZRBV_BLOB_INFO}" \
374	      || buc_die "Failed to find disk blob for ${z_needed_image}"
375	
376	    local z_blob_info
377	    IFS= read -r z_blob_info < "${ZRBV_BLOB_INFO}" || true
378	
379	    test -n "${z_blob_info}" || buc_die "No disk blob found in manifest for ${z_needed_image}"
380	
381	    local z_blob_digest="${z_blob_info%:*}"
382	    local z_temp="${z_blob_info#*:}"
383	    local z_media_type="${z_temp#*:}"
384	
385	    local z_extension="tar"
386	    case "${z_media_type}" in
387	      *zstd*) z_extension="tar.zst" ;;
388	      *gzip*) z_extension="tar.gz"  ;;
389	      *xz*)   z_extension="tar.xz"  ;;
390	    esac
391	
392	    echo "${z_extension}" > "${ZRBV_FACT_EXTENSION_PREFIX}${z_needed_image}"
393	
394	    local z_vm_blob_file="${ZRBV_VM_BLOB_PREFIX}${z_needed_image}.${z_extension}"
395	    podman machine ssh "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" --              \
396	        "crane blob ${z_source_fqin}@${z_blob_digest} > ${z_vm_blob_file}" \
397	      || buc_die "Failed to download blob for ${z_needed_image}"
398	
399	    local z_dockerfile_name="Dockerfile.${z_needed_image}"
400	    local z_dockerfile_path="${ZRBV_VM_TEMP_DIR}/${z_dockerfile_name}"
401	
402	    podman machine ssh "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" --             \
403	        "echo '${z_new_identity}' > ${ZRBV_VM_TEMP_DIR}/identity.txt" \
404	      || buc_die "Failed to create identity file"
405	
406	    {
407	        echo "FROM scratch"
408	        echo "COPY blob_${z_needed_image}.${z_extension} /disk-image.tar"
409	        echo "COPY identity.txt /identity.txt"
410	    } | podman machine ssh "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" -- \
411	        "cat > ${z_dockerfile_path}"                          \
412	      || buc_die "Failed to create Dockerfile for ${z_needed_image}"
413	
414	    local z_local_tag="localhost/podvm-${z_needed_image}:${z_new_identity}"
415	
416	    podman machine ssh "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" --                                  \
417	        "cd ${ZRBV_VM_TEMP_DIR} && podman build -f ${z_dockerfile_name} -t ${z_local_tag} ." \
418	      || buc_die "Failed to build image for ${z_needed_image}"
419	
420	  done
421	
422	  buc_step "All container images built"
423	
424	  buc_step "Tagging individual platform images..."
425	  for z_needed_image in ${RBRM_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS}; do
426	    local z_platform_tag="${ZRBV_VMIMAGE_TAG_PREFIX}${z_new_identity}-${z_needed_image}"
427	    local z_local_tag="localhost/podvm-${z_needed_image}:${z_new_identity}"
428	
429	    buc_step "Tagging ${z_needed_image} as ${z_platform_tag}"
430	    podman machine ssh "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" --             \
431	        "podman push ${z_local_tag} docker://${z_platform_tag}"         \
432	      || buc_die "Failed to push platform tag for ${z_needed_image}"
433	  done
434	
435	  buc_step "Update your RBRM configuration:"
436	  buc_code ""
437	  buc_code "# Add to rbmm_moorings/rbrm.env:"
438	  buc_code "RBRM_CHOSEN_IDENTITY=${z_new_identity}  # ${RBRM_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS}"
439	  buc_code ""
440	
441	  buc_success "Platform tags created for: ${RBRM_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS}"
442	}

444	# Fetch VM image from GHCR container
445	rbv_fetch() {
446	  # Handle documentation mode
447	  buc_doc_brief "Fetch platform-specific VM image from GHCR to local cache"
448	  buc_doc_lines "Pulls platform-specific container image by tag"
449	  buc_doc_lines "Extracts disk image to RBRS_VMIMAGE_CACHE_DIR"
450	  buc_doc_lines "Always overwrites existing cached image"
451	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
452	
453	  # Ensure module started
454	  zrbv_sentinel
455	
456	  # Perform command
457	  buc_step "Validating platform configuration..."
458	  test -n "${RBRS_VM_PLATFORM}" || buc_die "RBRS_VM_PLATFORM not set in station config"
459	
460	  buc_step "Ensuring cache directory exists..."
461	  mkdir -p "${RBRS_VMIMAGE_CACHE_DIR}" || buc_die "Failed to create cache directory"
462	
463	  buc_step "Starting ignite VM to access podman image cache..."
464	  zrbv_ignite_bootstrap false || buc_die "Failed to start ignite machine"
465	
466	  local z_platform_tag="${ZRBV_VMIMAGE_TAG_PREFIX}${RBRM_CHOSEN_IDENTITY}-${RBRS_VM_PLATFORM}"
467	
468	  buc_step "Pulling platform-specific container: ${z_platform_tag}"
469	  podman -c "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" pull "${z_platform_tag}" \
470	    || buc_die "Failed to pull platform container: ${z_platform_tag}"
471	
472	  buc_step "Creating temporary container from platform image..."
473	  local z_temp_container_id="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/platform_container_id.txt"
474	  podman -c "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" create "${z_platform_tag}" > "${z_temp_container_id}" \
475	    || buc_die "Failed to create temporary container"
476	
477	  local z_container_id
478	  z_container_id=$(<"${z_temp_container_id}")
479	
480	  buc_step "Setting up VM temporary dir..."
481	  podman machine ssh "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" --              \
482	      "rm -rf ${ZRBV_VM_TEMP_DIR} && mkdir -p ${ZRBV_VM_TEMP_DIR}" \
483	    || buc_die "Failed to create VM temp directory"
484	
485	  buc_step "Extracting disk image from container..."
486	  local z_vm_temp_disk="${ZRBV_VM_TEMP_DIR}/extracted_disk_image.tar"
487	  podman machine ssh "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" --             \
488	      "podman cp ${z_container_id}:/disk-image.tar ${z_vm_temp_disk}" \
489	    || buc_die "Failed to extract disk image from container"
490	
491	  local z_cache_file="${RBRS_VMIMAGE_CACHE_DIR}/${RBRS_VM_PLATFORM}-${RBRM_CHOSEN_IDENTITY}.tar"
492	  buc_step "Copying disk image to cache directory as -> ${z_cache_file}"
493	  podman machine ssh "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" -- \
494	      "cat ${z_vm_temp_disk}" > "${z_cache_file}"         \
495	    || buc_die "Failed to copy disk image to cache"
496	
497	  buc_step "Cleaning up temporary container..."
498	  podman -c "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" rm "${z_container_id}" \
499	    || buc_warn "Failed to remove temporary container"
500	
501	  buc_step "Removing temporary files in VM..."
502	  podman machine ssh "${RBRM_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}" -- \
503	      "rm -f ${z_vm_temp_disk}"                         \
504	    || buc_warn "Failed to remove temporary disk file"
505	
506	  buc_success "VM image cached: ${z_cache_file}"
507	}

509	rbv_init() {
510	  # Handle documentation mode
511	  buc_doc_brief "Initialize deploy VM from cached platform-specific image"
512	  buc_doc_lines "Uses cached disk image from RBRS_VMIMAGE_CACHE_DIR"
513	  buc_doc_lines "Initializes deploy VM with rootful mode"
514	  buc_doc_lines "Writes brand file with all RBRR_CHOSEN values"
515	  buc_doc_lines "Refuses if deploy VM already exists"
516	  buc_doc_lines "Requires rbv_fetch to be run first"
517	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
518	
519	  # Ensure module started
520	  zrbv_sentinel
521	
522	  # Perform command
523	  buc_step "Checking if deploy VM exists..."
524	  local z_machine_list
525	  z_machine_list=$(podman machine list) || buc_die "Failed to list podman machines"
526	  if [[ "${z_machine_list}" =~ ${RBRM_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME} ]]; then
527	    buc_die "Deploy VM already exists. Remove it first with rbv_nuke or manually"
528	  fi
529	
530	  buc_step "Validating platform configuration..."
531	  test -n "${RBRS_VM_PLATFORM}" || buc_die "RBRS_VM_PLATFORM not set in station config"
532	  case "${RBRM_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS}" in *"${RBRS_VM_PLATFORM}"*) ;; *)
533	    buc_die "Platform ${RBRS_VM_PLATFORM} not in manifest platforms: ${RBRM_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS}" ;; esac
534	
535	  local z_cache_file="${RBRS_VMIMAGE_CACHE_DIR}/${RBRS_VM_PLATFORM}-${RBRM_CHOSEN_IDENTITY}.tar"
536	
537	  buc_step "Checking for cached VM image..."
538	  test -f "${z_cache_file}" \
539	    || buc_die "VM image not found in cache: ${z_cache_file}" \
540	               "Run 'rbv_fetch' first to download the VM image"
541	
542	  buc_step "Initializing machine from cached image..."
543	  podman machine init --rootful --image "${z_cache_file}" "${RBRM_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME}" \
544	                                          2> "${ZRBV_DEPLOY_INIT_STDERR}"             \
545	       | "${ZRBV_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbupmis_Scrub.sh" "${ZRBV_DEPLOY_INIT_STDOUT}"             \
546	    || buc_die "Failed to initialize VM"
547	
548	  buc_step "Starting VM to write brand file..."
549	  podman machine start "${RBRM_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME}" || buc_die "Failed to start deploy VM"
550	
551	  buc_step "Generating brand file content..."
552	  local z_brand_file="${RBV_TEMP_DIR}/brand.txt"
553	  zrbv_generate_brand_file "${z_brand_file}" \
554	    || buc_die "Failed to generate brand file"
555	
556	  buc_step "Writing brand file to VM: ${ZRBV_EMPLACED_BRAND_FILE}"
557	  podman machine ssh "${RBRM_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME}" \
558	      "sudo tee ${ZRBV_EMPLACED_BRAND_FILE}" < "${z_brand_file}" > /dev/null \
559	    || buc_die "Failed to write brand file to VM"
560	
561	  buc_step "Stopping VM..."
562	  podman machine stop "${RBRM_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME}" \
563	    || buc_die "Failed to stop deploy VM"
564	
565	  buc_success "Deploy VM initialized with brand file"
566	}

568	rbv_start() {
569	  # Handle documentation mode
570	  buc_doc_brief "Start Deploy VM"
571	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
572	
573	  # Ensure module started
574	  zrbv_sentinel
575	
576	  # Perform command
577	  buc_step "Starting Deploy VM..."
578	  podman machine start "${RBRM_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME}"
579	
580	  buc_success "Deploy VM started"
581	}
582	
583	rbv_stop() {
584	  # Handle documentation mode
585	  buc_doc_brief "Stop Deploy VM"
586	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
587	
588	  # Ensure module started
589	  zrbv_sentinel
590	
591	  # Perform command
592	  buc_step "Stopping deploy VM..."
593	  podman machine stop "${RBRM_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME}"
594	
595	  buc_success "Deploy VM stopped"
596	}
597	
598	# eof
599	
600
